verses.6: The wounds of a friend are faithful,
verses.7: A full soul loathes a honeycomb;
verses.8: As a bird that wanders from her nest,
verses.9: Perfume and incense bring joy to the heart;
verses.10: Don’t forsake your friend and your father’s friend.
verses.12: A prudent man sees danger and takes refuge;
verses.13: Take his garment when he puts up collateral for a stranger.
verses.14: He who blesses his neighbor with a loud voice early in the morning,
verses.15: A continual dropping on a rainy day
verses.16: restraining her is like restraining the wind,
verses.18: Whoever tends the fig tree shall eat its fruit.
verses.20: Sheol and Abaddon are never satisfied;
verses.22: Though you grind a fool in a mortar with a pestle along with grain,
verses.23: Know well the state of your flocks,
verses.25: The hay is removed, and the new growth appears,
verses.26: The lambs are for your clothing,
verses.27: There will be plenty of goats’ milk for your food,
